danger_signals.meaning: Presence of corrupting supernatural threat to the world

danger_signals.visual_form: Death Combos with gory effects, clothing/hair severing
danger_signals.meaning: High-stakes lethal combat; consequences of failed defense are severe

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrative framing: 'When the night turns violent and the moon bleeds'
danger_signals.meaning: Cyclical threat; previous demon ruler fell to prophecy, current Demonlord Zarak faces same fate
World-state of martial law; champions must prove worth through combat to face ultimate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative setup: chosen warrior ('WeaponLord') will defeat demon
safety_signals.meaning: Despite danger, victory is cosmically ordained; structure implies survivability for righteous path

safety_signals.visual_form: One-on-one duel mechanics; Demonlord waits for proper challenger
safety_signals.meaning: Honor system active; not arbitrary death but structured contest with rules

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Demonic/skeletal imagery associated with supernatural rulers
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Zarak character appears as demon entity; visual distinction from human warriors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Battlefield setting; tournament arena representing conquered domain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Various mercenary and fighter characters with distinct weapon styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tournament bracket structure; progression through champion ranks
Prophecy as binding worldforce; destiny-driven narrative

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Raith defeats 20 rulers; establishes reign of terror

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shaman delivers oracle; narrative framing of cyclical threat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Raith chooses to wait for challenger rather than preemptively kill children; demonstrates confidence but seeds own downfall

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New Demonlord assumes power; 25-year gap indicates instability or succession crisis
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Zarak organizes tournament to identify and confront prophesied enemy; proactive rather than passive

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multiple armed fighters in combat progression; arenas as contested spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Warriors ascend through ranked combat; each victory demonstrates worthiness and martial prowess

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Narrative branching based on mercy vs. lethal force
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character ending sequences vary by opponent treatment (spared vs. Death Combo)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player choice of ruthlessness affects story outcome; moral dimension encoded in combat decisions

environmental_storytelling.example: Shaman's foretelling creates inevitable confrontation structure; players understand the world operates under supernatural destiny
Defeating 20 rulers (Raith's rise) and then facing champions in tournament (Zarak's regime) shows world hierarchy through combat sequence

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character weapon selection as faction/identity marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Each fighter wields distinct weapons; visual differentiation signals mercenary/warrior archetypes within the world

environmental_storytelling.technique: Death Combo gore as world violence indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Gory finishers visually communicate high stakes and brutal nature of this world's power struggles
Ability to resume 'in the middle of the story' reinforces that player is progressing through an ongoing conflict

entity_behavioral_tells.state: At peak power, confident in prophecy
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Refuses to preemptively kill children; waits for fair combat; demonstrates arrogance and belief in inevitable victory

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Newly consolidated power, aware of prophecy threat
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Organizes tournament proactively; controls arena; takes initiative rather than passive waiting

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Seeking to defeat demon or prove worth
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Engage in ritualized one-on-one combat; can choose mercy or lethal finishing; progress through ranked opponents
Progression toward Demonlord encounter; moral choices affect ending; thrust-blocking and counterattack mechanics emphasize active defense and readiness

notes: WeaponLord communicates world-state primarily through narrative framing (prophecy, succession, tournament structure) rather than environmental visual design. The fighting game paradigm constrains world-manifestation to character design, sprite animation, and combat mechanics. Visual danger signals rely on demonic character design and gore effects. Faction identity is signaled through character weapon distinctiveness rather than color/uniform coding typical of other genres. The world-state progresses through a cyclical threat pattern: demon possession → rise to power → prophecy issued → new demon inherits threat → tournament to identify hero. Player agency in deciding lethality (spared vs. Death Combo) creates branching narrative outcomes, encoding moral dimension into combat mechanics.
